summary.ascii_graph:
*-------.   Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).
|\ \ \ \ \  
* | | | | | Visit Wikipedia Main Page and extract at least 2 candidate next-step Wikipedia URLs from its outgoing links
 / / / / /  
* | | | | From Main Page candidates, choose one (e.g., Wikipedia:Contents) and extract the next Wikipedia URL from links on that page (step 2 of chain)
 / / / /  
* | | | Continue chain step: visit Help:Contents and extract next Wikipedia URL from its outgoing links (step 3 of chain)
 / / /  
* | | Continue chain step: visit Help:Introduction and capture explicit link evidence to the next page for verified adjacency pair near the end (step 4+ verification)
 / /  
* | Post-visit reflection: assemble ordered chain of ≥4 URLs and select an adjacency pair near the end; quote/link-scan evidence that URL[i] contains URL[i+1] among its links
 /  
* Merge: Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).

final_output.final_deliverable:
## Wikipedia URL chain (≥4), each step taken from links on the previous visited page

1) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page

2) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contents
   - Evidence that *Main_Page* links to *Wikipedia:Contents*: the Main Page’s link list includes `https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contents` (seen in the visited-page extraction for https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page).

3) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:Contents
   - Evidence that *Wikipedia:Contents* links to *Help:Contents*: the visited-page link list for https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contents includes `https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:Contents`.

4) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:Introduction
   - Evidence that *Help:Contents* links to *Help:Introduction*: on https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:Contents the text says: “for a friendly tutorial, see **our introduction**.” (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:Contents)


## Verified adjacency pair near the end (URL[3] → URL[4])

**Adjacency pair:**
- URL[3] = https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:Contents
- URL[4] = https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:Introduction

**Verification (quote from URL[3] showing the link):**
- On https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:Contents, in the “Edit an article” section, the page explicitly includes the link text “our introduction”:
  - Quote: “Contributing is easy: see how to edit a page. For a quick summary on participating, see contributing to Wikipedia, and for a friendly tutorial, see **our introduction**.” (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:Contents)

This “our introduction” link corresponds to the visited page https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:Introduction (as reflected in the extracted outgoing links for https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:Contents, which include `https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:Introduction`).


## Additional visit evidence (context quotes from pages in the chain)

- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page begins: “Welcome to Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia that anyone can edit.” (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page)
- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contents begins: “Easily explore Wikipedia using the topic links below.” (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Contents)
- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:Introduction begins: “This page takes you through a set of tutorials aimed at complete newcomers who wish to contribute.” (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:Introduction)
final_output.action_summary: Chain built by following links on each visited Wikipedia page: Main_Page → Wikipedia:Contents → Help:Contents → Help:Introduction. Verified adjacency near the end: Help:Contents links to Help:Introduction via the “our introduction” link (quoted from Help:Contents).
